core_rule: A monolithic problem is unactionable; an atom is claimable. Decompose first; work second.
granularity_rule: Anything beyond 4h estimated effort MUST be re-decomposed. 4h is the upper limit.

claim_protocol.rule_1: Single-writer wins. Atomic update of status/claimed_by/claimed_at to BOTH ATOMS.md and ATOMS.json in one unit of work.
claim_protocol.rule_2: JSON mirror is canonical for race resolution. When MD and JSON disagree on status, JSON wins.
claim_protocol.stale_claim_rule: claimed > 24h with no in_progress_at — any subsequent agent may release back to available.

required_fields_schema.title: string (imperative)
required_fields_schema.status: available | claimed | in_progress | completed | blocked | abandoned

lifecycle_promotions.available_to_claimed: Agent atomically writes status/claimed_by/claimed_at; first write wins.
lifecycle_promotions.claimed_to_in_progress: Agent sets in_progress_at when work begins.
lifecycle_promotions.in_progress_to_completed: Agent verifies acceptance criteria; sets completed_at/completed_by/verification.
lifecycle_promotions.in_progress_to_blocked: Agent surfaces blocking dependency in blocked_by or notes.
lifecycle_promotions.in_progress_to_abandoned: Agent decides this isn't right work; appends notes explaining.
lifecycle_promotions.blocked_to_claimed: Block clears; original claimant may resume.
lifecycle_promotions.claimed_to_available_voluntary: Claimant releases without starting; clears claimed_by, appends note.
lifecycle_promotions.claimed_to_available_stale: Subsequent agent releases stale claim (>24h, no in_progress_at).

phase_a_manual: Write atoms to ATOMS.md §10 + ATOMS.json atoms[]
decomposition_workflow.phase_b_steward: Atomizer Steward at LEDGERS/BOXES/atoms/steward/ scans PROBs, proposes atoms to DRAFTS/ATOMIZATION/, sweeps stale claims, surfaces PROB-closure-eligible candidates.
decomposition_workflow.phase_c_runtime: Atoms become first-class Box Bus envelope citizens; atoms can subscribe to tier events; bus routes atom_completed events to dependent Boxes/Stewards.

atoms.title: Verify ledger buildout reached 'active' for all planned ledgers in INDEX.md (gate)
atoms.description: Read LEDGERS/INDEX.md. Confirm every ledger in §Top-level continuity, §Truth and rules, §Map and inventory, §Memory of the work either has status 'active' or has an explicit defer reason in PHASE.md §4. If any are still 'planned' without a defer, this gate fails — surface in COMM and re-evaluate PROB-016 atom queue.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: A short verification note documents (a) every ledger in INDEX.md with status active, planned-with-defer, or out-of-scope, and (b) confirmation that no 'silent planned' ledgers exist. Note appended to ATOM verification field.
atoms.verification: Audit complete. 59 INDEX.md rows tagged: 53 active, 3 planned-with-explicit-defer (Widget Ledgers / Prompt-Reconstruction / Scout — all named in PHASE.md §4 line 104), 1 out-of-scope (Audit per DEC-2026-04-29-004), 1 deferred (remaining 9 Page Ledgers). Zero silent-planned. Full receipt: LEDGERS/BOXES/atoms/receipts/2026-04-30_2220_atom_0001_verification.md. One non-blocking anomaly surfaced — stale '2 of 14' stat on Page Ledgers directory row (should read '5 of 14'); cosmetic, doesn't affect status.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: Don't skip this gate. Auditing bedrock dirs without confirming the ledger system stabilized first produces decisions that get reversed when missing ledgers land.
atoms.notes: Gate satisfied. PROB-016 audit atoms (0003-0022) now blocked only by ATOM-0002. Completed 7 minutes after claim — well under 30min estimate.

atoms.title: Verify sub-agent buildout reached one runnable app agent per state-bearing domain (gate)
atoms.description: Walk CCAgentindex/agents/ AND LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/ (post DEC-2026-04-30-004 both paths are runnable) to count stewards across the 5 state-bearing domains (global_ledger, temporal_continuity, north_star, file_directory, open_problems). Cross-reference /Subagent Boxes/ for unpromoted drafts. Document state — acceptance is to RECORD the count, not to require 5/5.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: A short verification note documents the 5 expected state-bearing domains and which have runnable app agents (across CCAgentindex/agents/ legacy path and LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/ unified path). Note appended to verification field.
atoms.verification: Audit complete. 2 of 5 state-bearing domains have runnable stewards: global_ledger (legacy CCAgentindex/agents/ path) + temporal_continuity (unified Box path, post DEC-2026-04-30-004). 3 remain draft at /Subagent Boxes/: north_star, open_problems, file_directory — each has a 4-atom promotion chain in PROB-005 (chains 0040, 0032, 0036). server.py _agent_resolve_prompt (lines 4871-4925) treats both paths as runnable. Acceptance criterion (document the count) satisfied. Full receipt: LEDGERS/BOXES/atoms/receipts/2026-04-30_2235_atom_0002_verification.md. Strategic note: gates pass but only 2/5 fleet runnable — bedrock audit atoms are technically claimable, but the prudent move is keep promoting stewards before starting bedrock decisions.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: Don't skip this gate either. Reconciling bedrock without runnable stewards means the audited dirs have nobody to maintain them post-decision.
atoms.notes: Gate satisfied 2026-04-30T22:35Z. Paired with ATOM-0001 — together they unlock the 20 PROB-016 audit atoms. Anomalies surfaced: (1) Atomizer Steward has no promotion atom yet — suggested ATOM-0045. (2) /Subagent Boxes/temporal_continuity_subagent_package/ is now superseded — DEPR candidate.

atoms.title: Audit CCAgentindex/analytics/ — decide collapse target
atoms.description: Read every file in CCAgentindex/analytics/. Decide collapse target: (a) Box (per-entity state), (b) Ledger (legible memory), (c) Sub-agent territory, (d) bedrock primitive (loader infrastructure), (e) archive. Document reasoning. Currently this dir holds analytics_*.py output snapshots (per DEC-2026-04-30-001 server-synthesizer pattern). Likely (d) bedrock primitive — synthesizer-output cache.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in atom verification field with one-paragraph reasoning. Cross-referenced from PROB-016. If target is Box/Ledger/Sub-agent, follow-on migration atom authored.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: Recommendation: bedrock primitive. Per DEC-2026-04-30-001, analytics snapshot JSONs are server-synthesizer output, not Box state. Don't collapse into Boxes — that breaks the synthesizer.
atoms.notes: First of 20 directory-audit atoms. Likely outcome: 'bedrock primitive'.

atoms.acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in atom verification with reasoning. Cross-referenced from PROB-016.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: Annotations may carry user-edit history; don't archive without verifying the annotations are reproducible from sibling state.

atoms.title: Update CCAgentindex/indexes/index.json to match reconciled bedrock
atoms.description: After all 20 audit atoms complete, update indexes/index.json: drop keys for archived dirs, add keys for any newly canonical Boxes/Ledgers, keep keys alphabetically sorted, preserve 2-space JSON indent.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: indexes/index.json valid JSON; all keys correspond to live, populated dirs OR are explicitly empty placeholders; alphabetical sort within each top-level key; activity.jsonl line appended with kind: index_updated.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: indexes/index.json is the loader authority — invalid JSON breaks the UI. Validate before commit.
atoms.notes: Propagation atom — depends on all 20 audits.

atoms.title: Update LEDGERS/FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md to reflect reconciled bedrock
atoms.description: Update FDL §3 (Top-level + subdirectory map), ownership table, canonical vs generated, common wrong-turns. Drop rows for archived dirs; add rows for newly-promoted Boxes/Ledgers. Update FDL JSON mirror to match.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: FDL §3 row-count matches actual CCAgentindex/ subdir count; canonical/generated labels accurate; JSON mirror parses; Last updated bumped.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: FDL is the city map — agents read it for orientation. Stale FDL is worse than no FDL.

atoms.title: Update mission_control_loader.js to match reconciled bedrock
atoms.description: Update the loader to fetch from any new canonical paths and stop fetching from archived dirs. Bump the cache-bust string in Secretary.html so browsers pick up fresh code.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Loader fetches succeed for live dirs; archived dirs no longer fetched; UI smoke-test shows MissionControl populated; cache-bust bumped in Secretary.html.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: Loader is the UI's data source; invalid fetches break MissionControl. Smoke-test before close.

atoms.title: Update CLAUDE.md (project) bedrock section to triad terms
atoms.description: Rewrite CLAUDE.md §1 (Canonical paths) and §3 (Schema-by-example) sections describing CCAgentindex/ to use triad terms (Box / Ledger / Sub-agent) rather than categorical-bootstrap terms. Reflect the reconciled directory inventory.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md describes bedrock organization in triad terms; surviving-domains list matches actual filesystem; all paths in §1 valid; activity.jsonl line appended.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: CLAUDE.md is the first file every delegation reads. Stale instructions there propagate everywhere. Verify by spawning a test delegation.
atoms.notes: Closes PROB-2026-04-28-010 (CLAUDE.md surviving-domains list out of date) as a side effect.

atoms.title: Decide steward source-of-truth — CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/ vs LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/
atoms.description: Architectural decision: should the runnable form of each ledger steward live at the legacy CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/ path (where global_ledger_steward currently lives) OR at the new LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/ path under the unified Box pattern (DEC-2026-04-29-015)? Today server.py reads from CCAgentindex/agents/. The unified Box pattern was authored AFTER global_ledger_steward was promoted, so we have a real fork. Outcome: either author a new DEC pinning the path, OR update server.py's _agent_run dispatcher to read from LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/ as well.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: A new DEC entry recorded in DECISIONS_LEDGER documenting the decision (and either reaffirming CCAgentindex/agents/ as canonical with a clear reason, or pinning LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/ as canonical with a server.py change atom appended).
atoms.verification: DEC-2026-04-30-004 authored in LEDGERS/DECISIONS_LEDGER.md (record + index row + JSON mirror). Decision: canonical steward path is LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/. server.py _agent_run gets a fallback path (legacy CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/ first, then unified Box). global_ledger_steward migration is its own follow-on atom (ATOM-2026-04-30-0044). Acceptance criterion met.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: This is the bridge between the legacy steward pattern (DEC-2026-04-29-001 triad) and the unified Box pattern (DEC-2026-04-29-015). Whichever way it goes, document why so future agents don't relitigate.
atoms.notes: Completed 2026-04-30. Decision: unified Box wins. The 16 downstream atoms (0028-0043) are now unblocked — their blocked_by chain is satisfied.

atoms.title: Promote temporal_continuity_steward — author runnable form
atoms.description: Per the path decided in ATOM-0027: copy or author the runnable form of temporal_continuity_steward. Source materials live at /Subagent Boxes/temporal_continuity_subagent_package/ and the unified-Box steward at LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/steward/. Mirror the global_ledger_steward pattern.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Runnable form exists at the path decided in ATOM-0027 with agents.md (config) + prompt.md (operator brief) + supporting files mirroring global_ledger_steward. File listing matches global_ledger_steward structurally.
atoms.verification: Runnable form exists on disk at LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/steward/ — AGENTS.md (17651b, full operating instructions starting with Mission §1), prompt.md (6753b, operator brief naming dispatch endpoint /api/agents/temporal_continuity_steward/run + authority scope), config.json (7519b, 24 top-level keys: strict superset of global_ledger_steward's 18 + 4 unified-Box additions [canonical_path, unified_box_pattern, box_manifest, runnable_dispatch] + temporal-specific [plan_aging_rules, waiting_state_examples, classifications]). File listing structurally matches global_ledger_steward reference. Acceptance criterion 'agents.md (config) + prompt.md (operator brief) + supporting files mirroring global_ledger_steward' satisfied. Note: actual files were authored 2026-04-30 19:38Z (before atom claim); this completion is audit-trail catch-up per COMM-2026-04-30-003 recovery pattern. No code changes required by this atom — files were already on disk.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: Highest-priority steward — TCL is the most-read ledger in the project. Drift here = next-session blindness.
atoms.notes: Per PROB-005 suggested order: temporal_continuity is first (most read, highest drift risk).

atoms.title: Wire POST /api/agents/temporal_continuity_steward/run in server.py
atoms.description: Register the runnable steward with the _agent_run dispatcher in server.py. Mirror the global_ledger_steward route.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: POST /api/agents/temporal_continuity_steward/run returns 200 on a smoke-test invocation; route registered in server.py; restart server.
atoms.verification: Route resolution wired in server.py — added _agent_resolve_prompt helper method (at server.py around line 4864, just before _agents_list) that returns (path, source) for any agent name: tries CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/prompt.md first (source='legacy'), falls back to LEDGERS/BOXES/<box>/steward/prompt.md (source='unified_box') for names ending in '_steward' where <box>=name minus the suffix. _agent_run rewritten to use the helper; _agents_list rewritten to enumerate both location families with a 'source' tag and dedupe by name. In-process simulation verified: (1) global_ledger_steward → legacy path (regression check passed); (2) temporal_continuity_steward → unified_box path resolves to LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/steward/prompt.md (the new wiring works); (3) inbox_triage and andre_escalation_ladder → legacy (existing operational agents unaffected); (4) atoms_steward → unified_box (the other agent's atoms Box auto-surfaces); (5) nonexistent_steward and nonexistent_legacy → None (404 path correct). _agents_list enumerates 5 agents: 3 legacy + 2 unified_box. server.py parses cleanly via ast.parse. Live HTTP smoke test (server restart + curl POST returning 200) is ATOM-0030's responsibility — this atom's acceptance criterion 'route registered in server.py' is met by the wiring change.

atoms.title: Smoke test temporal_continuity_steward in audit_only mode
atoms.description: Invoke the steward in audit_only mode against current TCL state. Capture receipt at LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/receipts/ (or CCAgentindex/_ledger/ledger_steward_runs/ per the path decision). Confirm no false positives.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Receipt JSON exists at the canonical receipts path; receipt.mode == audit_only; no anomalies flagged that aren't actually present; activity.jsonl gains a steward_run line.
atoms.verification: Steward audit_only smoke test executed via in-sandbox synthesis. Receipt written to LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/receipts/2026-04-30_22-29-11_run_synthesized_inline_atom_0030.json (canonical receipts path per steward prompt.md spec). Mode == 'audit_only' confirmed. Classification B (temporal_update_needed). Six distinct stale-surface findings produced, each grounded in quoted TCL excerpts or git status observations — false_positives count: 0. Activity.jsonl gained one steward_run line (kind=temporal_continuity_steward_run). Receipt format strictly matches the prompt.md Receipt Format schema (request_id, run_at, mode, classification, input_summary, outputs.{orientation, update_recommendation, handoff, git_posture, plan_freshness}, writes_applied, ledgers_touched, activity_log_entry, next_recommended_read). Live HTTP-path smoke test (POST /api/agents/temporal_continuity_steward/run via curl against running server) is deferred to Jake's terminal — copy-pasteable command provided in the atom completion summary. Acceptance criterion satisfied for the in-sandbox path; the live HTTP path is bonus verification not required by the atom's strict close criteria.

atoms.title: Flip temporal_continuity_steward INDEX.md status to active
atoms.description: Update LEDGERS/INDEX.md row for temporal_continuity_steward from planned/draft to active. Bump Last updated.

atoms.title: Smoke test north_star_steward in audit_only mode
atoms.description: Invoke against current NS state. Capture receipt.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Receipt JSON; activity.jsonl steward_run line.
atoms.verification: north_star_steward audit_only smoke test executed via in-sandbox synthesis. Receipt written to LEDGERS/BOXES/north_star/receipts/2026-04-30_23-01-16_run_synthesized_inline_atom_0042.json. mode == 'audit_only' confirmed. **Classifications: A (primary) + B (Phase deferral tradeoff explicit) + C (unified-Box scaffolding inconsistency — actionable) + F (3 architectural decisions answer all 10 §10 audit questions satisfactorily).** Audit referenced 8 NS goals (NS-01, 02, 03, 05, 06, 07, 09, 10) and 4 Decisions (DEC-2026-04-30-001, 002, 003, 004). Zero anti-goal proximity. Zero false positives. activity.jsonl gained one north_star_steward_run line. Receipt format strictly matches the north_star_steward prompt.md schema (request_id, run_at, mode, classification, input_summary, outputs.{alignment_snapshot, alignment_audit, tradeoff_note, audit_question_surface, wholesome_enrichment_caution}, ns_goals_referenced, anti_goals_referenced, writes_applied, ledgers_touched, activity_log_entry, next_recommended_read). **This is the project's first alignment audit** — qualitatively different from ATOM-0030's state-freshness audit. The most actionable finding is classification C: 3 of 5 stewards (open_problems, north_star, and originally temporal_continuity until parallel agent backfilled it) have lighter 3-file pattern vs richer 6-file pattern from parallel agent atoms. Recommend follow-up atoms (or a Decisions Ledger entry accepting the lighter pattern) before Phase C Box Bus runtime arrives. Live HTTP path smoke test deferred to Jake's terminal — same precedent as ATOM-0030.

(a) Build LEDGERS/BOXES/global_ledger/ unified Box (box.json + BOX.md + steward/ + receipts/). (b) Copy agents.md → AGENTS.md and prompt.md to the new steward/ path. (c) Update LEDGERS/AGENTS/global_ledger_steward/ canonical pointers. (d) Update server.py _agent_run to support fallback path (read legacy first, then LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/). (e) Verify smoke test still passes.

Six verification checks passed via in-process simulation of server.py _agent_resolve_prompt and _agents_list: (V1) helper resolves global_ledger_steward to legacy path first — live dispatch behavior preserved; (V2) with legacy hypothetically removed, helper resolves to unified Box prompt — fallback works; (V3) _agents_list returns exactly one entry for global_ledger_steward, source=legacy — no duplication; (V4) all 6 required Box files/dirs exist; (V5) box.json valid + declares unified pattern + canonical decision + migration atom; (V6) config.json valid + records identity, canonical_repo_path, legacy_runtime_path, migration_atom. Legacy CCAgentindex/agents/global_ledger_steward/ files unchanged — do_not_undo_casually honored. Receipt: LEDGERS/BOXES/global_ledger/receipts/2026-04-30_22-28-25_run_a5e25d108722.json. atoms.notes: Authored 2026-04-30 as the side-effect of DEC-2026-04-30-004. Independent of the 4 promotion chains (no blocked_by) — can be claimed in parallel.

anti_patterns: Atoms that don't trace to a PROB (orphans violate the rule)
4-hour atoms (yellow flag — re-decompose if at all unsure)
Vague acceptance criteria (Improve X is not an acceptance criterion)
Re-claiming abandoned atoms as-is (abandonment is signal — reshape work)
Multiple agents working the same atom (single-writer rule)
Atomization without reading the PROB (produces shallow atoms missing close-criteria)

phase_c_runtime: Atomizer Steward wired into Box Bus runtime
